notes: ADB is a policy/infrastructure funder, not a route data keeper. Routes in this file represent rail corridors financed by ADB and the aggregate statistics cited in ADB documents. No bus or jeepney route-level data is published by ADB.

routes.route_name: North-South Commuter Railway — South Commuter Railway Project (SCRP)
routes.frequency_notes: Under construction; scheduled completion 2030+ (part of NSCR). 18 stations total along 54.6km corridor.
routes.notes: ADB financing up to $4.3B multitranche. 18 stations; connections to all existing LRT/MRT lines and future Metro Manila Subway. Source: ADB project 51117 and SCRP page. Station list is partial — only 12 of 18 documented from public sources.

routes.frequency_notes: Under development; no schedule published. 10 stations along ~13.4km elevated corridor.
routes.notes: ADB financing under development (Project 53172-001). Will interconnect with MRT-3 and Metro Manila Subway. Source: ADB project page. Station names and exact alignment not yet published; stops listed are estimated from corridor description (eastern Metro Manila / Marcos Highway area).

routes.frequency_notes: Current: 3–5 min headway peak; 5–8 min off-peak. Rehab targets full 54-train fleet deployment.
routes.notes: ADB serving as PPP transaction advisor for DOTr on private operator procurement (2024). 17km, 13 stations along EDSA. Source: ADB advisory announcement 2024. Fare cited from existing MRT-3 operational data, not ADB publication.

aggregate_statistics.note: These statistics are cited in ADB publications but do not correspond to individual route records
aggregate_statistics.source: ADB Transport Sector Assessment (adb.org/sites/default/files/institutional-document/33700/files/philippines-transport-assessment.pdf) and ATO Metro Manila Urban Transport Profile (December 2024)
